ambiguities.description: Source and target systems, their APIs, and capabilities
Identity mapping and key resolution between systems
Conflict resolution strategy (last-write-wins, merge, manual, custom)
Retry, idempotency, and exactly-once delivery semantics
Rate limits, quotas, and throttling from external systems
Observability requirements for sync health and failures
Credential and secret management for external system access
Backfills, replays, and historical sync scope
Failure semantics (fail-stop, partial success, degrade gracefully)

questions.question: What are the source and target systems?
How are identities mapped and resolved between systems?
What schema transformations and validations are required?
What conflict resolution strategy is required?
What rate limits and quotas must be respected?
What reconciliation strategy and cadence are needed?
What observability is required for sync health?
How are credentials and secrets managed?
What backfill or replay scope is required?

assumptions.assumption: Source and target systems expose stable APIs or data interfaces
Standard retry with exponential backoff is sufficient for transient failures

suggested_closures.decision: Integration hub construction will separate connectivity, transformation, sync orchestration, and observability into independent layers.
suggested_closures.rationale: Separation enables adding new connectors without rewriting transformation logic, and supports independent scaling of sync and observability.

seed_tasks.title: Inventory source and target systems and their APIs
seed_tasks.transformation: List all systems, their APIs, auth methods, rate limits, and data models.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: System inventory with API documentation exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every system has documented API endpoints, auth, and rate limits.

seed_tasks.title: Define sync direction and identity mapping
seed_tasks.transformation: Document sync direction per entity and how keys are mapped between systems.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Sync direction and identity mapping are documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every entity has defined direction and a resolvable identity mapping.

seed_tasks.title: Design transformation, validation, and conflict resolution
seed_tasks.transformation: Document schema transformations, validation rules, and conflict resolution policy.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Transformation and conflict resolution policies are documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every sync path has defined transformation rules and conflict handling.

residuals.description: Source and target systems are not identified.
Conflict resolution strategy is not selected.
